preamble: The shift from a voiced dental fricative [ð] to a rhotic [r] or tap [ɾ] is a form of rhotacism, typically occurring in intervocalic positions where the fricative weakens into a resonant sound.

examples.note: Common in the Bressan dialect where historical dental sounds shifted toward rhotic realizations.
Occurs in some dialects where the dental fricative is realized as a tap [ɾ] or rhotic [r].
